quick_facts.label: Conducting organisation
quick_facts.value: LIC AAO is a recruitment process conducted by the Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) for Assistant Administrative Officer posts; the official 2025 recruitment covered Generalist and Specialist AAO streams.

quick_facts.label: Most recent official reference cycle
quick_facts.value: LIC’s Careers page records the 2025 AAO recruitment, including examination results and lists of candidates shortlisted after pre-recruitment medical examination. Candidates should not assume an annual 2026 cycle or reuse 2025 dates, vacancies, eligibility, fees, or pattern unless LIC issues a new notice.

quick_facts.label: Selection stages
quick_facts.value: For AAO Generalist in the 2025 notice, selection comprised preliminary examination, main examination, interview, and subsequent pre-recruitment medical examination. Preliminary marks were not used in final merit; main-exam and interview marks were used for final merit.

quick_facts.label: Generalist eligibility snapshot
quick_facts.value: In the 2025 Generalist notification, applicants needed to be 21–30 years old as of August 1, 2025 and hold a bachelor’s degree in any discipline from a recognised Indian university or institution. The notice provided category-specific upper-age relaxations.

quick_facts.label: Application fee context
quick_facts.value: For the 2025 Generalist cycle, the non-refundable online charge was ₹85 plus transaction charges and GST for SC/ST/PwBD candidates, and ₹700 plus transaction charges and GST for other candidates. This was cycle-specific and may change in a future notification.

quick_facts.label: 2025 Generalist pay context
quick_facts.value: The 2025 notice stated starting basic pay of ₹88,635 per month and approximate minimum-scale total emoluments of ₹1,26,000 per month in an A-class city, inclusive of applicable allowances. These figures were explicitly subject to LIC rules and city classification and should be treated as dated context rather than a current guarantee.

sections.heading: What LIC AAO is
paragraphs: LIC AAO is LIC’s officer-level recruitment route for Assistant Administrative Officers. It is not a permanent admission test or a reliably annual examination: LIC publishes recruitment-specific notices through its Careers portal, which define the available streams, vacancies, eligibility, schedule, assessment structure, and service conditions for that cycle. The official 2025 page covered AAO Generalists and several Specialist categories, alongside Assistant Engineer recruitment.

For applicants, the practical implication is to identify the exact post named in the live notice. “AAO Generalist” and AAO specialist posts can have materially different educational and assessment requirements; specialist roles in the 2025 cycle included Chartered Accountant, Company Secretary, Actuarial, Legal, Insurance Specialist, and engineering-related recruitment streams.
bullets: Use LIC’s Careers page as the controlling source for whether an application window is open and for all cycle-specific instructions.
Do not treat third-party “expected” dates, cut-offs, vacancies, or syllabi as official until they appear in LIC’s notice or candidate handout.

sections.heading: Eligibility: use the post-specific notification
paragraphs: For the 2025 AAO Generalist recruitment, the baseline requirement was a bachelor’s degree in any discipline from a recognised Indian university or institution. The age band was 21 to 30 years as of August 1, 2025. LIC also listed upper-age relaxations, including 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, and specified relaxations for PwBD, eligible ex-servicemen/commissioned officers, and confirmed LIC employees. These dates and limits belong to the 2025 cycle and must be rechecked for any later recruitment.

Specialist AAO eligibility was not interchangeable with Generalist eligibility. For example, the 2025 Chartered Accountant post required a bachelor’s degree, ICAI Final qualification, completed articleship, and ICAI associate membership; other specialist posts had their own qualifications and, in some cases, different maximum ages. LIC stated that equivalent qualifications would not be accepted unless specifically prescribed, and that supporting evidence is verified during the process.
bullets: First choose the notified post/discipline; then read its age, qualification, experience, category, and disability provisions in full.
Ensure the qualifying result date and documents meet the notice’s stated cut-off; eligibility is provisional at application and subject to document verification.
Applicants seeking a reserved category or age relaxation should preserve the prescribed, valid supporting certificates required by LIC.

sections.heading: Exam and selection format
paragraphs: The 2025 AAO Generalist process had three competitive stages—online preliminary examination, online main examination, and interview—followed by pre-recruitment medical examination for candidates reaching final merit. Preliminary marks did not count towards final merit. LIC used main-examination marks to shortlist candidates for interview, and used main-examination plus interview marks for final merit.

In the 2025 Generalist preliminary exam, the online objective test was one hour with separate 20-minute sections: Reasoning Ability (35 questions/35 marks), Quantitative Aptitude (35/35), and English Language (30 questions/30 marks). The notice described English as qualifying, with its marks not counted for ranking; it also stated that there was no negative marking in the Generalist preliminary examination.

The 2025 Generalist main exam comprised objective tests totalling 300 marks—Reasoning Ability, General Knowledge/Current Affairs, Data Analysis & Interpretation, and Insurance and Financial Market Awareness—plus a 25-mark English descriptive paper. The descriptive component assessed communication tasks such as emails, reports, situation analysis, and precis writing; it was qualifying and its marks were not counted for ranking. LIC reserved the right to modify examination structure in a future cycle.
bullets: Prepare from the current notification and official handout for the specific AAO stream, not from a generic AAO pattern.
Treat sectional qualifying standards and shortlisting ratios as notice-specific: LIC can set or revise them for a recruitment cycle.
Specialist and Legal streams in the 2025 recruitment used different preliminary subjects and main-exam professional-knowledge components.

sections.heading: How to apply when LIC opens a cycle
paragraphs: In the 2025 Generalist cycle, applications were online only through LIC’s website and Careers recruitment link. Candidates created a new registration using their name, contact details, and email address, received a provisional registration number and password, completed the application, uploaded required files, paid the online charge, and were advised to retain the system-generated application printout. A future cycle may use a different portal, dates, document specifications, or payment amount.

The 2025 notice required candidates to have a valid personal email address and mobile number active through the recruitment process, and to upload a photograph, signature, left-thumb impression, and handwritten declaration in the specified format. For examination entry, the notice required the call letter, original valid photo identity proof, and a photocopy of that identification. LIC directed candidates to download call letters through its Careers site, generally from seven days before the online examination.
bullets: Apply only through the official LIC Careers recruitment link published for the live cycle.
Read the live notice before payment: fees are non-refundable and the eligibility conditions are enforced even after registration.
Check the uploaded images, name consistency, contact information, category selection, and final preview before submission; preserve the completed application and payment proof.

sections.heading: Preparation aligned to the official scheme
paragraphs: Build preparation around the post-specific official scheme. For the 2025 AAO Generalist notice, that means timed practice for preliminary Reasoning Ability, Quantitative Aptitude, and English Language; then deeper main-exam practice in Reasoning, General Knowledge and Current Affairs, Data Analysis & Interpretation, and Insurance and Financial Market Awareness. Include typed English communication practice because the main descriptive component covered emails, reports, situation analysis, and precis writing.

Because the official notification gives separate timing for sections and requires candidates to qualify section-wise, use timed sectional practice as well as full-length simulations. For specialist AAO posts, replace a generic Generalist plan with the professional-knowledge and descriptive requirements stated for the relevant discipline. Before the examination, download and follow LIC’s official handout and call-letter instructions, since LIC may modify the structure and issues detailed candidate instructions through its website.
bullets: Create a syllabus checklist directly from the current LIC notice and official handout.
Practise computer-based descriptive responses, rather than only handwritten essays, where the notification requires typed answers.
Track insurance and financial-market awareness for the notified Generalist main exam, but avoid assuming that prior-cycle topics or marks will recur unchanged.

sections.heading: What selection leads to
paragraphs: Successful candidates in the 2025 AAO Generalist process were considered for regular LIC posts, subject to final merit, document checks, and medical fitness. The notice said selected candidates could be posted or transferred anywhere in India according to LIC requirements, noted that many vacancies were in mofussil branches, and stated that candidates would be posted for at least five years in mofussil branches outside their home zones after selection and successful training. These are 2025 service conditions and can change in a later recruitment.

For the 2025 Generalist appointment, LIC specified one year of probation, extendable up to two years, and a minimum four-year service undertaking including probation. Leaving before that period could trigger ₹5 lakh liquidated damages under the guarantee bond described in the notice. The official Careers page subsequently published lists of candidates shortlisted after pre-recruitment medical examination, illustrating that an examination result alone was not the final appointment step.
bullets: Final selection requires more than clearing an online exam: candidates must satisfy interview, merit, document, and medical requirements.
Review posting, probation, bond, pay, and benefits terms in the exact notice before deciding whether to accept an offer.
Treat all compensation and service-condition figures as dated notice-specific context.
